what_it_does: This file is a channel-attachment metadata record that associates attachment id att-3129ad27 with the dragnet-development channel, declares its attachment_type as board_items, and points its target to the board item board://item-a76dc14f, recording an added timestamp.
observations: Only YAML frontmatter is present; there is no body content.
The attachment id in the frontmatter matches the filename stem.
The target is expressed as a board:// URI referencing item-a76dc14f.
The added timestamp is an ISO 8601 value with timezone offset.
conventions: Filename derived from the attachment id.
References an external artifact via a URI scheme rather than a local path.
concerns: No descriptive text explains why the board item is attached.
No author or provenance beyond the timestamp.
The semantics of board_items are not defined in the file.
notes: A bare provenance record linking a dragnet-development channel attachment to a specific board item.
questions: What content or state does board://item-a76dc14f represent?
Why was this board item attached to the channel memory?
What does attachment_type board_items imply about format or handling?
Who added the attachment and on what trigger?
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, timestamped association between a channel memory entry and a board item, so the attachment can be retrieved by id and traced back to its source board item.
novelty: The target uses a board:// URI scheme and the file contains no body content, functioning purely as a typed pointer.
contribution: It contributes a stable, typed, timestamped link (att-3129ad27) from the dragnet-development channel to board item a76dc14f, which a fused system could use to resolve or group channel attachments with board artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record for dragnet-development that links attachment att-3129ad27 to board item board://item-a76dc14f with type board_items and an added timestamp, containing no body content.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to record that board item a76dc14f is attached to the dragnet-development channel memory at a specific point in time.
